rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM ROUSE VS. CHEVRON CORP. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, May 20, 2010, line 1, DEFENDANT EXXON MOBIL CORPORATION DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT. Is sustained without leave to amend. Late opposition considered. Plaintiff fails to allege that Exxon is responsible for his damages. Plaintiff does not identify the allegedly injury-producing toxin, exposure to a sample manufactured or supplied by Exxon, or allege how the toxin entered his body. See Bockrath vs. Aldrich Chemical Co. (1999) 21 Cal. 4th 71.) Fraud is not allged with specificity. No facts establish extreme or outrageous conduct by Exxon to support emotional distress claim. No reasonable probability that defects can be cured by amendment. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: INFORMATION DELIVERY SERVICES INC VS. ATM EQUITY PARTNERS, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, May 20, 2010, line 3, DEFENDANTS ATM EQUITY PARTNERS, LLC, CRAIG WICHNER DEMURRER. Demurrer sustained with 10 days leave to amend. The Court does not consider extrinsic evidence not properly subject to judicial notice in considering a demurrer. However, as the 1st and 2nd causes of action, plaintiff must allege false representations with specificity, and representations must be statements of fact, not opinions or future predictions. Plaintiff must specify who made the mispresentations, to whom, in what capacity, and when. Plaintiff must plead defendants acted with requisite intent. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: SONG HUA XU et al VS. BING JUAN CAO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, May 20, 2010, line 9, DEFENDANTS BING CAO, and, JIE LIA MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Motion denied. Defendants fail to show that the gravamen of the remaining causes of action arise from petitioning activities. Defendants may bring a motion for attorney fees regarding the 15th and 16th causes of action. Those causes of action arose from petitioning activity and plaintiffs cannot avoid liability for fees by dismissing those causes of action. =(302/CWW)
